classifications.phrase: decided to raise the cash rate target by 25 basis points
Inflation in Australia has passed its peak but is still too high
is proving more persistent than expected a few months ago
progress looks to be slower than earlier expected
an increase in interest rates was warranted today
the risk of inflation remaining higher for longer has increased
Underlying inflation was higher than expected
the economy is experiencing a period of below-trend growth
Conditions in the labour market have eased but they remain tight
the economy is forecast to grow below trend
employment is expected to grow slower than the labour force
Returning inflation to target within a reasonable timeframe remains the Boards priority
The Board remains resolute in its determination to return inflation to target

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a hawkish stance dominated by inflation concerns, with a 25bp rate increase justified by persistent inflation above target and upside surprises, partially offset by dovish signals regarding below-trend growth and labour market easing.
